name: Doberman Pinscher
description: Created in the 1890s by German tax collector Karl Friedrich Louis Dobermann, who wanted a loyal and intimidating protection dog for his rounds collecting taxes in rough neighborhoods, the Doberman Pinscher was purpose-built for intelligence and courage. Modern Dobermans have been selectively bred toward a more stable, family-friendly temperament while fully retaining their natural alertness and trainability, consistently ranking among the most responsive and biddable of all breeds. Dilated cardiomyopathy is a serious breed-wide concern requiring regular echocardiographic screening.

conditions_detail.name: Dilated Cardiomyopathy (DCM)
conditions_detail.description: DCM is a disease of the heart muscle causing the chambers to enlarge and the walls to thin, reducing the heart's pumping efficiency. It progresses to congestive heart failure if untreated and is a leading cause of cardiac death in large breeds.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Management with diuretics, ACE inhibitors, Pimobendan, and antiarrhythmics can prolong life significantly. No cure exists; treatment is palliative.
prevention_tips: Annual cardiac screening for at-risk breeds
Avoid grain-free diets in predisposed breeds (FDA advisory)
Taurine supplementation investigated in some breeds

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Wobbler Syndrome (Cervical Spondylomyelopathy)
conditions_detail.description: Wobbler syndrome is spinal cord compression in the neck caused by malformed or unstable vertebrae or herniated discs. It produces a characteristic wobbly gait in the hindlimbs and can progress to complete paralysis.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Medical management with strict rest, anti-inflammatories, and physiotherapy is used for mild cases. Surgical decompression and stabilisation is recommended for moderate to severe neurological deficits.
prevention_tips: Avoid rapid growth in predisposed breeds

conditions_detail.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions_detail.description: Hypothyroidism is the most common endocrine disorder in dogs, caused by reduced thyroid hormone production — most often from immune-mediated thyroiditis or idiopathic follicular atrophy. It affects metabolism, skin, and coat quality.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Daily oral thyroxine (levothyroxine) supplementation is highly effective and required for life. Dosing is adjusted based on thyroid level monitoring every 6-12 months.
prevention_tips: Regular blood screening in at-risk breeds from age 4
